summary_one_line: A %d/%i integer conversion whose decimal value is negative (or with a +/space sign flag), making the sign-prefix branch true

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The fuzz input must form a format directive routed to FORMAT_INT decimal conversion (base 10) whose signed value is negative, so is_neg becomes 1 and the sign-prefix guard at line 780 takes its true side; this requires a coherent %d/%i spec with an argument byte sequence decoding to a negative integer.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry a structured header/URL-like prefix (e.g. bytes 00 01 00 00 00 19 then a 'WS'/'po' tag, ':/', '127.0.0.1:9001', '850') that drives the parser into the decimal-integer arm; the W-only overlay shows lines 738-741 (negative-to-positive conversion) and 781 (--width) hit, and the DIVERGENT BRANCHES table shows line 735/736 reaching is_neg true (T=5) and line 787 OUTCHAR('-') reached only under W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (34-36 bytes) and dominated by literal '%'/'A'/'+' bytes (e.g. %`%a, %A, %C@%, %%Cb%ca) that route into the FLAGS_CHAR ('%c') arm and string formatting rather than a base-10 integer with a negative value; the L overlay never reaches lines 738-741 or 781, and line 736 is_neg shows T=0 F=40 for L.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes intercepted CMP operands into the input at matching offsets, letting it construct the precise parser-routing bytes (tag/colon/path/numeric fields) that produce a negative decimal integer; naive lacks CMP interception and must hit this multi-byte structural condition by blind havoc, which it does only 1/10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the sign-prefix guard combining is_neg with FLAGS_SHOWSIGN/FLAGS_SPACE in the integer-formatting path, true side globally blocked.
The branch lies in the FORMAT_INT decimal arm where is_neg is derived from a negative signed value.
Only winner seeds reach the negative-to-positive conversion block and the --width sign-prefix consequence.
Only the winner reaches the negative-sign output, confirming is_neg taken true under W.
Winner-resolving seeds contain a structured header/URL-like prefix with tag, colon, path and numeric fields.
The is_neg-true side is reached only in the winner role at line 736 (W T=5 vs L T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 736  T=5 F=20  T=0 F=40  if(is_neg) {

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds are short and dominated by literal percent/char bytes rather than a numeric negative argument.
The loser exercises the FLAGS_CHAR arm extensively (L T=163) while the winner does not.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 703  T=0 F=60  T=163 F=167  if(p->flags & FLAGS_CHAR) {

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one intercepted CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and relies only on havoc/token mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 291 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 6543e87c49a8de08` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 12 and 13 of the 14-level ancestor chain

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose %d argument is forced to a negative value (is_neg=1 unconditionally) shows naive resolving the line-780 true side as fast as cmplog, i.e. no I2S advantage when the negative-integer structural condition is removed.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-level claim that winner seeds decode specifically to a negative decimal integer is inferred from the parser-routing prefix and W-only coverage of lines 738-741/788 rather than from a directly observed negative argument value.
